

- # **CODING NOTES:**

  - **Store cookies for open/collapse Profile, filter preferences**  
    - **Temporary for browser session**  
  - **Have temporary table to store temporary increase/decrease in health during the year → reset at end of year**  
  - **have function to calculate if you can afford something, params: cost, current money; returns boolean**  
  - **Shouldn’t have to pay dental fees while on parents’ insurance**  
  - **Add “create game” button homepage (already in Figma)**  
  - **No tax prep fee if don’t have job**  
  - **For CC→bachelors, if doing bachelors in same major as associates, can do in 2 yrs, get half of bachelors gains (including Physics associates → Astronomy bachelors); if doing bachelors in different major, must do 4 yrs**  
  - **Enforce savings withdrawal limit when paying on expenses page**  
  - **Does accountant pay tax prep fee? No, put notice that accountant don’t pay fee**  
  - **Need to track both your and spouse’s vehicles**  
    - **Car cards may apply to your or spouse’s vehicle**  
    - **Pay insurance, gas, etc for both cars**  
    - **Track age for repairs, etc. for both cars**  
  - **Loans:**  
    - **If single, loans \-\> orig\_loans, accrue interest annually**  
    - **If married, your loans and spouse loans remain in orig\_loans, accrue interest annually; loans variable is for joint loans acquired while married, accrue interest annually \- show both player’s own loans and join loans separately**  
    - **Min payment for spouse loans automatically paid each year; you can choose if you want to additionally  pay off your own loans, joint loans, or more of spouse loans**  
  - **How to display taxes calculation (‘i’ icon)?**  
    - **tell them what bracket they’re in**  
    - **Include link to tax brackets table (create own in google doc)?**  
  - **At end of year, reevaluate childcare situation (num kids in childcare age range and how much time) \-\> at beginning of year, if anything changed, give announcement that they may want to change their childcare**  
  - **Add any yearly stress to expenses page (school, job, kids, etc.)**  
  - **Check health requirement every year for jobs (fired if don’t meet) for jobs that have a health requirement**  
    - **Grace period of 1 year if fall below requirements (and notify), then fired if still below**  
  - **Check degree level for job’s salary:**  
    - **\*check highest one first (ex. PhD, then masters)**  
  - **Apply action filters in real-time**  
    - **Each time there is a change in your cart (adding actions, taking actions out, changing dropdowns, etc), auto-refresh filters to account for new eligibility**  
    - **Sorting**  
      - **lemons/time block (descending)**  
      - **lemons/$ (descending)**  
      - **cost/time block (ascending)**  
      - **Minimum cost (ascending)**  
    - **Filters list**  
      - **Category**  
        - **Mental Health**  
        - **Physical Health**  
        - **Social Connections**  
        - **Family**  
        - **Entertainment**  
        - **Outdoors**  
        - **Animals**  
        - **Education (broadly college-related stuff)**  
        - **Schoolwork (specifically academic actions)**  
        - **Career**  
          - **Author Career**  
          - **Musician Career**  
          - **Actor Career**  
        - **Luxury**  
        - **Home & Auto**  
        - **Community**  
        - **Skill/Trait Development**  
        - **Other**  
      - **Decreases stress (checkbox \- not checked by default)**  
      - **Doesn’t increase stress (checkbox \- not checked by default)**  
      - **Increases health (checkbox)**  
      - **Earns money (checkbox \- not checked by default)**  
      - **Only show me things I’m qualified for (checkbox \- not checked by default)**  
      - **Max cost total**  
      - **Free**  
      - **Min/max time blocks**  
      - **Min lemons**  
      - **Skills/traits (checkbox)**  
      - **Only show my favorites**  
      - **Good deed lemon opportunity**  
      - **Things I will no longer be eligible for soon (within the next 5 years)**  
      - **Increases available time blocks (stay up late, request PTO, change school FT to PT (if would increase time blocks), childcare (if would increase time blocks))**  
      - **Has senior discount**  
      - **Actions I did last year**  
      - **Actions I haven’t done**  
      - **Requires PTO**  
      - **College-only**  
      - **Schoolwork**  
      - **For grandparents**  
  - **on housing/vehicles, page automatically sort by cost low-\>high**  
    - **Additional filters for housing:**  
      - **Location**  
      - **Recommended max people (include info buttons for what this means)**  
      - **Legal max people (include info buttons for what this means)**  
      - **Pet limits**  
      - **Rent vs. buy (2 checkboxes)**  
      - **Max cost**  
    - **Compare feature for housing/vehicles**  
    - **Additional filters for vehicles:**  
      - **Max cost**  
      - **Min people**  
      - **Cars vs. non-cars**  
      - **Car age (info button with significance of car age \- more issues, higher insurance, etc)**  
      - **Fuel type (gas, hybrid, electric)**  
  - **jobs page**  
    - **sort: salary, pto, time blocks, stress**  
    - **Filter:**  
      - **Salary**  
      - **PTO**  
      - **Time blocks**  
      - **Stress**  
      - **Degree**  
      - **Major**  
      - **skill/trait requirements**  
      - **skill/trait gains**  
      - **Has additional perks**  
      - **Raise type**  
      - **Jobs I meet the requirements for**  
      - **Has salary increases with additional degrees**  
      - **Location**  
  - **parent contributions**  
    - **If they gave you a car, we can just automatically give it to you and put it in your cart; otherwise, can’t checkout first year until buy transportation**  
    - **If paying for college, can just put a note on the school action where the cost is that parents are paying for $X**  
    - **For age kicking you out of house, can put on “living with parents” house card that must move out by age X**  
    - **For wedding contribution, put on Dating app action that getting married will cost you $X (before opening Dating app action)**  
  - **Include base college reqs in edu reqs**  
  - **when add edu reqs to DB, put all possible edus that lead to that degree if they can serve as the previous degree (ex: if next step can be met with Associates or Bachelors, list both; if must have Masters, then only list Masters)**  
  - **when add job edu\_reqs to DB, put all possible edus that lead to that job if they can serve as the min req (ex: if can have job with Associates or Bachelors, list both; if must have Masters, then only list Masters)**  
  - **For transportation, if insurance \== null, do insurance calculation (put “i” with factors)**  
  - **For bike, restrict bike can’t go between areas**  
  - **When picking card, add all card ids to list (with appropriate frequency and randomly pick)**  
  - **Spouse can only have 1 job**  
  - **Spouse can go school PT and have job PT**  
  - **Spouse cpr cert auto-renews if they require it but still pay for it (listed on expenses page)**  
  - **Track edu job yrs**  
  - **When graduate from school, show pop up to congratulate them and remind them to get a job**  
  - **Include bike/public transit in new transportation, don’t include insurance/gas in cost filter**  
  - **Switching jobs in the middle of a raise cycle**  
    - **Make sure raises and total PTO reset**

# **MISCELLANEOUS**

- **Name of game: Lemonade (When life gives you lemons…)**

# **GAMEPLAY**

- **No taking turns, everyone can play out the year synchronously, once last person finishes year (completes expenses) → next year starts**  
- **Players notified when new year has started (Happy Birthday pop-up and noise)**

## **START OF GAME**

- **Community pitcher starts with no lemonade, no line until year 2**  
- **Parent contributions**  
  - **Notified about all of these**  
  - **Car, paying for college, kick out of house, wedding contribution**  
  - **Roll die:**  
    - **1: No car, no college $, cannot live in Parents’ Place, $0 for wedding**  
    - **2: No car, $5000 college, cannot live in Parents’ Place, $5000 for wedding**  
    - **3: No car, $15000 college, cannot live in Parents’ Place after age 22, $7500 for wedding**  
    - **4: 10-yr-old affordable 5-seater gas car, $30000 college, cannot live in Parents’ Place after age 22, $10000 for wedding**  
    - **5: 5-yr-old affordable 5-seater gas car, $45000 college, cannot live in Parents’ Place after age 26, $12500 for wedding**  
    - **6: new affordable 5-seater gas car, $60000 college, cannot live in Parents’ Place after age 30, $15000 for wedding**  
    - **7: No car, $60000 college, cannot live in Parents’ Place after age 22, $0 for wedding**  
    - **8: No car, no college $, cannot live in Parents’ Place, $15000 for wedding**  
    - **9: Bike, no college $, can always live in Parents’ Place as long as meet requirements (num people), $0 for wedding**  
    - **10: luxury 2-seater electric car, $100000 for college, cannot live in Parents’ Place, $15000 for wedding**  
- **At beginning of game, to see how much $ have out of HS, 3-part system:**  
1. **Personal savings: Random number generated between $0 and $5000 (skewed right)**  
2. **Parent wealth (use same number rolled above)**  
   1. **Parent contribution roll \= 1, add $0**  
   2. **Parent contribution roll \= 2, add $100**  
   3. **Parent contribution roll \= 3, add $1000**  
   4. **Parent contribution roll \= 4, add $5000**  
   5. **Parent contribution roll \= 5, add $1000**  
   6. **Parent contribution roll \= 6, add $10000**  
   7. **Parent contribution roll \= 7, add $100**  
   8. **Parent contribution roll \= 8, add $0**  
   9. **Parent contribution roll \= 9, add $100**  
   10. **Parent contribution roll \= 10, add $25000**  
3. **Check their starting organization skill level**  
   1. **Organization 0-45: decrease by 20% (min \= $0)**  
   2. **Organization 46-90: increase by 20%**  
- **Chronic health condition:**  
  - **20% chance of starting game with chronic health condition**

## **BEGINNING OF YEAR**

- **Get paid for that year’s job at beginning of year (ex: if you switch jobs that year, you have the previous job that whole year and got paid your full salary, and then you actually switch jobs the following year)**  
- **At beginning of year, when calculate current stress (job, college, card, etc.)**  
  - **if high stress tolerance (67-100%), decrease stress 15%**  
  - **If medium stress tolerance (34-66%), no change**  
  - **If low stress tolerance (\<34%), increase stress 10%**  
  - **Put in instructions that stress tolerance can increase/decrease stress**  
- **Debt stress: \+1% stress per $20k in debt**  
- **Expenses stress:**  
  - **Calculate mandatory expenses for next year (i.e., not voluntary actions they might do)**  
  - **Add their salary for next year to their remaining money from this year**  
  - **Subtract the mandatory expenses from the salary \+ money**  
  - **If the final amount:**  
    - **\<= 1000 \=\> \+5% stress**  
    - **\<= 5000 \=\> \+3% stress**  
    - **\<= 10000 \=\> \+1% stress**  
- **Each year, if your pet(s) are in the age range to die, roll determines if they die**  
  - **Roll for each individual pet, multiple can die in same year**  
  - **50-50 chance**  
- **Popup for when CPR certification has expired**  
  - **Also put in notifications and recommended actions**  
- **At beginning of year, roll to see if got grandchild**  
  - **Frequency Formula: (1/5) \* \# kids, then roll 5-sided die and if 1 through \# kids shows up on die, then success**  
  - **only kids between 25 and 40 are included in formula**  
- **Check stress**  
  - **Affects health for next year**  
  - **If health is \>= 70%**  
    - **if stress is above 90%**  
      - **health goes down 2%**  
    - **if stress is above 80%**  
      - **health goes down 1%**  
  - **If health is \>= 50% and \< 70%**  
    - **if stress is above 85%**  
      - **health goes down 3%**  
    - **if stress is above 75%**  
      - **health goes down 2%**  
  - **If health is \< 50%**  
    - **if stress is above 80%**  
      - **health goes down 4%**  
    - **if stress is above 70%**  
      - **health goes down 3%**

## **Finances**

- **Show health, stress net gain**  
- **Loans (can get loans and pay them off on the finances page)**  
  - **Automated minimum loan payment: 5%/yr (if they don’t have enough money, automatically give them a loan to cover that amount and put a note/alert in that section telling them that they are getting the additional loan)**  
  - **Interest rate on all loans is 8%**  
  - **If can’t afford something, need to get a loan \- can’t go into negative money (flash notification that need to get loan, you close it)**  
- **Tax prep fee**  
  - **If you have 1 job & age \< 65 & (income \< 200,000 filing jointly or income \< 100,000 filing single) & no loans \=\> filing simple tax return \= free**  
  - **Otherwise, pay $1600 for tax prep fee**  
  - **If you or spouse have accounting experience, do not have to pay tax prep fee**  
- **Childcare**  
  - **Year FT: $12000/yr/child**  
  - **Year PT: $6000/yr/child**  
  - **Year PT \+ Summer FT: $7500/yr/child**  
  - **Summer FT: $3000/yr/child**  
  - **Summer PT: $1500/yr/child**  
- **Chronic health condition costs (per chronic health condition)**  
  - **With health insurance: $3000/yr**  
  - **Without health insurance: $5000/yr**  
- **Tuition (see College section)**  
- **Housing (rent, utilities \- see Housing sheet)**  
  - **Toggle insurance**  
- **Transportation costs (gas, insurance \- see Transportation sheet)**  
  - **Maintenance fees: vehicle age 0-2 \= $1100, 3-4 \= $1200, 5-7 \= 1300, 8-10 \= 1400, 11+ \= 1500**  
    - **Excludes bike/public transit**  
    - **Mechanic discount \= 80% off**	  
  - **Public transit is yearly cost per person**  
- **Toggle health insurance (voluntary, on parents’ insurance til age 26\)**  
  - **Single: $6000/yr \+ $300/yr (standard increase due to age)**  
  - **Family: $12000/yr \+ $1000/child \+ $450/yr (standard increase due to age/kid injuries)**  
  - **All kids under 18 must be on your health insurance plan; kids roll off plan once they turn 26; but once all kids turn 18 and if you are divorced, you can get a single plan**  
- **Groceries**  
  - **$3000/person/yr (only including kids if they are under 18\)**  
  - **If at least 3 people in family (only including kids if they are under 18), buy in bulk → only $2400/person/yr (save 20%)**  
- **Annual pet expenses (food, etc): $300/small pet, $500/large pet**  
  - **Vet fees: $75/small pet, $1000/large pet**  
- **Child expenses (kids \<18): $11000/child/yr**  
- **Miscellaneous (you and your spouse): $1200/person/yr**  
- **Retirement fund**  
- **College fund**  
  - **Have option to contribute amount of current money to children’s college fund**  
  - **Gain 1 lemon/% of current money**  
  - **Children go to college from ages 18-22, cannot contribute once last kid reaches age 22**  
- **End of year finances can be paid from regular money or retirement savings (enter amount for each in textbox)**  
- **Inflation (cost goes up every year by random percentage in range):**  
  - **Housing: 4-7%**  
  - **Salaries: 2-5%**  
  - **Auto insurance: 2-5%**  
  - **Home insurance: 7-9%**  
  - **Other (ex: actions like zoo ticket): 0.1-0.3%**  
  - **Tax brackets: every 5 years, income brackets shift up $15000 (first one still has min of $0)**  
- **Taxes**  
- **Spouses file jointly**  
- **Following US progressive income tax system:**

**![][image1]**  
**![][image2]**

## **RETIREMENT**

- **retirement savings account, contribute at end of year, play through retirement, can use both regular money and retirement savings in retirement, retirement earns 5%/yr, voluntary, no savings goal, no min/max you can contribute per year, no min balance required, pay end of year from whichever account they want (or both in whatever proportion they want)**  
  - **If you are 65+ years old, you are considered “retired” even if you still have a job and your retirement account is accessible without penalty**  
  - **While under 65, can contribute but limitations on using it (penalty); while retired, can still contribute and can use freely**  
  - **If want to access retirement savings before retirement, there is a penalty of 10% additional tax for following year**  
  - **Spouse automatically retire at 65 (if you didn’t make them retire already), player cannot force them to get a job after 65**  
  - **Notifications for when spouse will be retiring and for when retirement savings are available for use without penalty**  
- **Don’t have to retire, can work forever**

## Dying

- **Lose health over time per chart below**

		

| Age range | % health lost/yr | Est. health remaining naturally, can increase with health actions |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **40s** | **1%** | **60-90** |
| **50s** | **2%** | **40-70** |
| **60s** | **3%** | **10-40** |
| **70s** | **4%** | **0** |
| **80s** | **5%** | **0** |

- **20% chance of dying every year once reach 5%**  
- **Put in instructions that health goes down over time and eventually you will die**

# **GAME COMPONENTS**

## **USER INTERFACE**

## **Navbar**

* **Health, stress, money**  
* **Player name**  
* **Game name and logo**  
* **Profile quickview (opens drawer)**  
* **Notifications icon with small drawer on click to list notifications**  
* **On expenses and actions pages, at top of page, have accordion with notifications so it’s more prominent**   
  * **Example notifications:**  
    * **things you have to take into account this year (health too low for job, upgrading house/car, etc)**

  


### **Pages**

- ## **Home (start/join game, instructions)**

- ## **Profile**

  - **Show 1 big skills progress bar with each skill plotted**  
  - **Show 1 big traits progress bar with each trait plotted**  
  - **Show all relevant life things**  
  - **Notes feature:**  
    - **Can type, speech-to-text, draw, place items on timeline**  
- **Actions (Squeeze the Day\!)**  
- **Finances (with past trend analysis & future forecasting)**  
- **Plan (page to test out different life scenarios)**  
- **Jobs**  
- **Education**  
- **Transportation**  
- **Housing**  
- **Pitcher (also show mini view in nav bar with tooltip)**  
- **Messaging system (available on every page)**  
- **Scrapbook (badges \= Lemon Grove)**  
  - **Add feature to review history of significant life events when game ends? (Ex: at age 18, started Bachelors in art, at age 22 graduated with BA in art, at age X bought X house, at age X got married, at age X had kid, etc.)**  
  - **Track all actions they do and show them how many times they did each one (don’t show ones they did 0 times)**  
    - **Pie chart of types of activities**  
  - **Also track grandkids and tell them how many**  
  - **How many lemons they earned over the course of their life**  
  - **Graph of how much money you had over time**  
  - **Starting and ending skills and traits**  
  - **Inflation of different categories over time**

## **PROFILES**

- **Traits (all bell curves unless otherwise specified)**  
  - **starting:**  
    - **Bravery (random assign 0-80)**  
    - **Perseverance (random assign 0-80)**  
    - **Charisma (random assign 0-100)**  
    - **Compassion (random assign 10-100)**  
    - **Creativity (random assign 10-100)**  
    - **Organization (random assign 0-90)**  
    - **Patience (random assign 0-100)**  
    - **Caution (random assign 0-100)**   
    - **Sociability (random assign 20-100)**  
    - **Stress Tolerance (random assign 0-100)**  
    - **Good with kids (0-80)**  
    - **Physical ability (0-100)**  
    - **Communication (10-80) \- bell curve**  
- **Skills**  
  - **starting:**  
    - **Math (2-10) \- bell curve**  
    - **Science (2-10) \- bell curve**  
    - **Art (0-10) \- skewed right**  
    - **Music (0-10) \- skewed right**  
    - **Writing (2-10) \- bell curve**  
    - **Analysis (2-10) \- bell curve**  
    - **Home repair (0-10) \- skewed right**  
    - **Technology (2-10) \- bell curve**  
- **At beginning of game, random assign traits and skills → they get a certain number/percentage of traits/skills to increase, we show them job and college requirements when they’re picking so they can make sure they’re qualified for what they want, show requirements but also highlight or somehow indicate at a glance what they can already do, can both increase and decrease traits/skills, a % decrease add that % onto your available increases**  
  - **How much can they adjust their traits/skills at beginning of game**  
    - **Traits: can increase traits 50% total (max of 10%/trait)**  
    - **Skills: can increase skills 10% (max of 2%/skill)**

    

## **HEALTH & STRESS**

- **Bars for health and stress**  
- **Stress affects health**  
- **Build health, stress: health starts 70-100% (skewed left), stress starts at 0% each yr → add stress for job/school//etc.**  
  - **If low health \= more likely health prob**  
  - **Certain Actions \= stress change**  
- **People with chronic illnesses have a max health of 100% \- (15% \* num chronic health conditions) (so no matter how many health actions they do, they don’t get fully healthy)**  
  - **When show Health in toolbar, still show as 0-100, but gray out after their max (ex: 80, so the scale stays at 0-100 but really it’s 0-80 for them) (tooltip with reason why health limit is lower \= chronic illness)**  
  - **When do health action, only get 80% of health gained, rounded up to the nearest whole number if a decimal**  
    - **On action results, show what they would actually gain**  
- **For temp/permanent health changes (cards & actions), tell them how much health decreased, but not what proportion is temporary/permanent**  
- **Permanent health loss decreases max health**  
  - **When show Health in toolbar, still show as 0-100, but gray out after their max (ex: 80, so the scale stays at 0-100 but really it’s 0-80 for them) (tooltip with reason why health limit is lower \= permanent health damage from XYZ)**

## **COMMUNITY LEMONADE PITCHER**

* **Actions give lemons that go into the community pitcher**  
* **Line in the pitcher goes up every year, if the community cannot add enough lemons in to meet the new line, the game ends (one-year grace period)**  
* **By the end of the game, the pitcher must be full**  
* **No line for first couple years (i.e., lemon goal starts at age 20\)**  
* **Number of lemons to fill pitcher is multiple of number of players (need to calculate each year in case people died and they are no longer counted players in the game)**  
  * **Ages 20-22 \= 10 lemons/person**  
  * **Ages 23-30 \= 20 lemons/person**  
  * **Ages 31-50 \= 40 lemons/person**  
  * **Ages 51-65 \= 60 lemons/person**  
  * **Ages 66-end \= 80 lemons/person**  
  * **Add up total to get pitcher size, then each year show goal line for that age group**  
* **Have option for player to leave game (or others can kick them out) → the goal for a full pitcher adjusts accordingly (their lemons they already put in stay there)**  
* **Pitcher shows current number of lemons in pitcher, goal number to reach for this year, number of lemons to go to get to goal, and recommended num of lemons for each player to get to support reaching that goal**  
* **Good deeds lemons:**   
  * **when a player picks a card with a good deeds lemon effect, all other players get a pop-up asking if they want to perform a deed (options dependent on the card) and earn 2 lemons \* multiplier which is based on \# good deeds you’ve done over course of game (i.e., every 5 good deeds you’ve done  \= \+1 multiplier) or not do a deed and lose 1 lemon \* multiplier which is based on \# bad deeds you’ve done over course of game  (i.e., every 5 bad deeds you’ve done  \= \+1 multiplier)**  
  * **When player does action with good deeds lemon effect,  earn action result lemons \* multiplier which is based on \# good deeds you’ve done over course of game (i.e., every 5 good deeds you’ve done  \= \+1 multiplier)**  
  * **Each year, shows all players list of 3 same good deeds they can do and they can each select 1 to do that year and earn 2 lemons \* their good deed lemon multiplier**  
    * **Options:**  
      * **Help an old lady cross the street**  
      * **Open the door for someone**  
      * **Read to kids at public library**  
      * **Pick up trash at park**  
      * **Let someone go ahead of you in line**  
      * **Give a compliment**  
      * **Walk dogs at the animal shelter**  
      * **Send a card to a service member**  
      * **Tell someone why they are special to you**  
      * **Do yard work for a neighbor**  
      * **Volunteer at a soup kitchen**  
      * **Spend quality time with a loved one**  
      * **Volunteer at community garden**

## **TIME BLOCKS**

- **Actions Page Time Block (TB) Scales:**  
  - **Possible sections: max of 60 time blocks total for all sections together**  
    - **Sleep: there by default \= 20 tb (if do stay up late action, decrease Sleep tb and increase Actions tb)**  
    - **Work: only there if have job \= \# tb for job (if use PTO, decrease Job tb and increase Actions tb)**  
      - **PTO: if do request PTO action, convert work time blocks to PTO time blocks**  
    - **Kids: only there if have kids under 18 \= \# tb taking care of kids (if do childcare action, decrease Kids tb and increase Actions tb)**  
    - **Commute: only there is commute for job**
    - **Pets: only there if have pets**
    - **Activities: there by default \= remaining tb after other sections**  
  - **Show scale at top of Actions page to display how you are using your time**  
  - **Only show 1 time off bucket (either PTO or time off (unpaid (ex: teacher summer)))**  
    - **If PTO:**  
      - **Must use PTO action to request X time blocks off (max of half your job’s time blocks) (only use one PTO action per year so put all PTO needed in that one)**  
      - **Then put actions that require PTO in your cart**   
      - **Replace job time blocks in the “how i spend my time” slots with time off time blocks you are using**  
    - **If unpaid time off:**  
      - **Job scale only goes up to job time blocks and activities scale is time off \+ activities time blocks**  
  - **![][image3]**  
  - **![][image4]**

## **COLLEGE**

* **Can go at any age**  
* **Vocational is $7500/yr**  
* **Associates is $5000/yr**  
* **Bachelors is $15000/yr**  
* **Professional schools:**  
  * **Police Academy: $5000/yr**  
  * **Teaching Credential: $15000/yr**  
  * **Firefighter Academy: $5000**  
  * **Flight School: $80000/yr**  
  * **Law School: $30000/yr**  
  * **Astronaut Academy: $0/yr**  
  * **Pharmacy School: $15000/yr**  
  * **Med School: $50000/yr**  
  * **Chiropractic School: $20000/yr**  
  * **Dental School: $40000/yr**  
  * **Optometry School: $30000/yr**  
  * **Physical Therapy School: $15000/yr**  
* **Masters is $30000/yr (except AI joint masters/PhD degree)**  
* **PhD is $10000/yr (including AI joint masters/PhD degree)**  
* **Part-time tuition is half FT tuition per year**  
* **Scholarship action**  
  * **Everyone can apply for scholarships, don’t tell them criteria but only receive one if they meet the criteria**  
  * **Extra scholarship money (after paying year’s tuition) rolls over to next year until graduate with that degree, then surplus scholarship money goes away**  
  * **Show scholarship money bank in finances**  
* **Can pay with any combination of parent contributions (automatic), own money, scholarships, and/or loans**  
  * **Do in end of year**  
  * **First is scholarships, then parent college contribution, then can decide how much of own money and how much loans**  
* **Stress: based on academic actions**  
* **\* major \= real major (nursing degree, music degree, etc)**  
* **\* field \= department (math, humanities, health)**  
* **Everyone can apply for college, if you don’t have the skills you will get rejected and receive a hint about what you need to improve on to get in**  
* **Drop out: can’t afford, got enough skills for job that doesn’t require degree, changed mind about career**   
* **College: if job requires degree, must go to college; otherwise, not required if meet min. skills for a desired job; if choose to go to college to build skills, pick major, when graduate can get job based on skills**  
  * **if internship and job, don’t work during summer (no pay)**  
    * **If do internship in summer, only receive ¾ of job pay \=\> store flag in db that did internship for action that year and then check if also have job**  
  * **Every year, check if meet grad reqs, if so can graduate, if not stay in school another year**  
  * **Part-time college \= twice as long as FT**  
    * **Part-time \= use up ½ as many time blocks/yr for academic actions, 1/2 college stress/yr (round everything up)**  
* **Grad school: options are restricted based on degrees you have (see edu sheet)**  
* **Each year, choose part-time or full-time, gain set percentage of skills required for major every year (on Edu sheet at top)**  
  * **Ex: Bachelors: full-time \= 4 yrs gain 5%/yr \= 20% total, part-time \= 8 yrs gain 5 \* 0.5 \= 2.5%/yr \= 20% total**  
  * **there will be a minimum amount of skills to graduate but they can do extra actions (internships) if they want a job that will need higher skills**  
  * **To graduate, need 1 year Gen Ed classes, 1 year Field class, 2 year Major classes (double for PT) (need to tell them this so they know last time they can switch majors without having to extend schooling)**  
    * **This doesn’t matter if you graduate early (Gen Ed is already completed before start that degree → don’t need to do 4 years)**  
  * **Can switch major**  
    * **Gen Ed classes always transfer, Field classes transfer if in same field, Major classes don’t transfer**  
    * **Once Gen Ed and Field classes are complete, they stay complete (don’t have to re-do them when getting another degree)**  
    * **If switching back to a major previously started but not completed, most recently completed classes for that major must have been done within last five years, otherwise need to redo whole major (ex: if doing major and drop out and then go back to school, if completed courses in that major within last 5 yrs, they still count, but if more than 5 yrs, have to redo)**  
* **For degrees that require actions beyond academic actions to graduate (to build additional skills), tell players this when they choose their major**  
* **Joining campus clubs**  
  * **Automatically stay in club for next school year (need to manually remove from cart)**  
  * **All build 5% sociability**  
  * **Specify type of club: (tell them what skills they gain but not how much):**  
    * **Art: 2% art, 5% creative**  
    * **Science: 2% science**  
    * **Creative writing: 2% writing, 5% creativity**   
    * **Robotics: 2% science, 2% technology, 2% home repair**  
    * **Band/Orchestra: 2% music**  
    * **Theater: 2% music, 5% charisma, 5% bravery**  
    * **Model UN: 5% communication**  
    * **Intramural sports: 5% physical ability**  
    * **CS: 2% technology**  
    * **Finance: 2% math, 5% organization**  
* **Graduation requirements for associates degree is half the bachelors requirements**  
  * **If go to bachelors after associates, Gen Ed and Field classes are completed already**  
* **For AI PhD, use masters entry requirements, but gain both masters and PhD automatically gained skills/traits: add masters and PhD amounts gained and divide by \# yrs of school**  
* **Include page in app for Education: only show fields, degrees, num years to complete, and job options**  
* **From player perspective, there is only Associates, Bachelors, Masters, PhD, and Specialization schooling options (but behind-the scenes Specialization is Vocational and Professional Schools)**  
* **you only gain the specified amount of skills if you do academic actions during the year, academic actions automatically at the top of actions page and stay there as you scroll**  
  * **Average perseverance and organization to figure out studiousness (low, average, high)**   
    * **Divide following ranges by 3 for low, avg, high for that level**  
      * **Associates: 0-100**  
      * **Bachelors: 30-100**  
      * **Masters/Professional: 50-100**  
      * **PhD: 70-100**  
    * **“Students of your studiousness typically have to do X time blocks of academic actions per year to stay on track to graduate. Those who persevere and are organized tend to have to do less.”**  
  * **Associates/bachelors: add up to 16 (high), 20 (average), 24 (low) time blocks**  
    * **Attend classes: 8**  
    * **Study: 4**  
    * **Complete coursework: 8**  
    * **Research: 4**  
    * **Go to office hours: 2**  
    * **Go to review session: 2**  
  * **Masters/Professional School: 16 (high), 20 (average), 24 (low) time blocks**  
    * **Attend classes: 2**  
    * **Study: 2**  
    * **Go to office hours: 4**  
    * **Research: 8**  
    * **Work on thesis: 8**  
  * **PhD: 20 (high), 24 (average), 28 (low) time blocks**  
    * **Publish a paper: 6**  
    * **Speak at a conference: 2**  
    * **Research: 10**  
    * **Work on dissertation: 10**  
  * **Vocational: 16 time blocks**  
    * **Attend classes: 8**  
    * **Complete coursework: 8**


## **JOBS**

- **In first year of new job, add 5% to job stress**  
- **For jobs available in city and suburbs (except rideshare driver), must pick where you are working**  
  - **Can switch location on job item in cart (let them know about any side effects like commute going up or ineligible for certain things)**  
- **PT jobs \= no PTO**  
  - **If accumulate PTO while FT at a easjob, then go PT at that same job, lose all PTO accumulated (tell player this will happen)**  
- **Determine eligible jobs based on skills/traits/degrees**  
- **On jobs page show what skills needed for job and degree if required**  
- **Once qualified for yrs experience jobs (i.e. cashier-\>store manager, etc.), always qualified**  
  - **Do not have to be consecutive yrs experience**  
- **Job listings:**  
  - **Specify whether need no degree or certain major**  
- **Some jobs have full time (FT) and part time (PT) options**  
  - **Part-time gets 50% of skills gained/yr, still need to meet full job requirements**  
  - **Part-time \= use up half as many time blocks, half job stress**  
- **Multiple people can have the same job**  
- **Edu careers:**  
  - **School counselor: Masters in edu**  
  - **Teacher: bachelors in anything \+ teaching credential**  
  - **Professor: PhD in anything**  
  - **Principal: masters in edu**  
  - **Dean: PhD in edu**  
- **Time Off (PTO or unpaid time off):**  
  - **PTO carries over, but unpaid time off doesn’t**  
  - **Every 10 years, PTO increases by 5 days (only for jobs that get PTO)**  
    - **Excludes:**  
      - **Flight attendant (have their own system on sheet)**  
  - **No limit on carryover if unused**  
  - **Don’t keep PTO if leave job → if leave job, reset PTO to 0**  
- **Jobs that have health requirements: if go below job’s health requirement, notice on actions page to either retire, switch jobs, or do health actions to meet requirement**  
- **If get job you previously had again:**  
  - **If job requires degree, must do continuing education (for every 2 years out of that job, must do 1 time block of continuing education)**  
  - **Get salary you left off at \+ inflation for years you have been gone (no raises)**  
- **Raise percentages do not increase with inflation**  
- **Pensions**  
  - **See Jobs sheet for eligible jobs**  
  - **Each year, you are prompted for how much you want to contribute to your pension that year. It comes out of your paycheck and employer matches up to 5% of salary (i.e, if i contribute 1% of my salary, employer also contributes 1% of my salary; if i contribute 6% of my salary, employer only contributes 5% of my salary)**  
  - **Pensions earn 5-9% interest annually (tell players it earns 7% on average)**  
  - **On Expenses page, show original salary, the deduction for pension contribution, and remaining salary to put towards expenses**  
  - **If you have/had multiple jobs with pensions, the pensions get combined.**  
- **Employer pays for part of health insurance**  
  - **FT: employer pays 75% (i.e., take off 75% of our health insurance costs listed in this doc)**  
  - **PT:**   
    - **Can go on spouse’s health insurance if they have family plan**    
    - **Employer plan (use single/family plans for FT): employer pays 25%**  
- **Actor has to audition for roles**  
  - **Movies only last for one year (20% chance of sequel the following year if have high skills: charisma \>=70, creativity \>= 70\)**  
    - **Pop-up notifies them they can star in sequel: accept or decline**  
      - **If accept, goes into their cart with results of action, but does not include audition time blocks**  
  - **TV shows last until the show is randomly canceled one year**  
    - **TV show season time blocks don’t include audition time blocks (results of action happen every year until show cancelled)**  
    - **Track how long you have been on show, percent chance of cancellation increases over time**  
      - **Cancellation chance:**  
        - **\+10% for each year you have been on the show**  
    - **Option to leave show (can take out of cart except during first season)**  
      - **In cart, displayed as “TV Show \#\_\_: Season X”**  
  - **Get notified immediately if got the role**  
  - **Can choose to get agent for each audition**  
    - **Tell them the agent will increase their chances of getting a role but don’t tell them what their original chance percentage is**  
    - **Chance increase is 20% (they will not know this percentage)**  
    - **If audition successful, agent gets 15% commission of pay**  
  - **Acting traits are bravery, charisma, and creativity**  
  - **To be successful at audition, add up those 3 traits**  
    - **220-247 (beginner): 20% chance of success**  
    - **248-273 (intermediate): 35% chance of success**  
    - **274-300 (advanced): 50% chance of success**  
  - **Range of salary based on traits**  
    - **Lower bound y=(0.78)1.048^x**  
    - **Upper bound y=(0.1)1.0725^x**  
    - **Pick a random number in this range**  
- **Singer/musician**  
  - **Music traits are music and creativity**  
  - **Earnings for actions:**  
    - **EP/album**  
      - **To determine which salary scale, add up those 2 traits**  
        - **100-133 (beginner): $0-$10000, skewed right**  
        - **134-167 (intermediate): $9000-$100000, normal curve**  
        - **168-200 (advanced): $50000-$10000000, skewed left**  
        - **Pick a random number in this range**  
    - **Opening tour:**  
      - **Charisma \<= 80: $40000**  
      - **Charisma \<= 90: $60000**  
      - **Charisma  \> 90: $80000**  
    - **National/International**  
      - **$75 mil \- 300 mil (pick random \#, double for international)**

- **Author**  
  - **Self-publish action salary**  
    - **Add writing \+ creativity:**  
      - **\<150: $25000**  
        - **\>=150: $50000**  
    - **Publisher action salary**  
      - **100k \- 1 mil (pick a random number)**

## **SPOUSES**

- **KISS: spouse \= no say in kids, school (you tell spouse to go to a certain school, major is based on education interest (field), job is assigned based on major, roll for scholarship every year \= 1 ($0), 2 ($1000), 3 ($5000), 4, ($10000), 5 ($15000), 6 ($20000)), divorce, etc**  
  - **Spouse graduates from school in default time frame**  
    - **Can go FT or PT, but must stick with it the whole time**  
    - **Cannot drop out**  
- **Spouse scholarships carry over to next year if extra money**  
- **Marriage compatibility score: calculate each year by adding up the following**  
  - **Did not have debt stress for past 2 years? score \+ 2**  
  - **Stress \< 90? score \+ 1**  
  - **Have kids under 18 and do at least 1 family action total over past 2 years? score \+ 2**  
  - **Don’t have kids under 18 and do at least 2 family actions total over past 2 years? score \+ 2**  
  - **Compassion \>= 80? score \+ 1**  
  - **Patience \>= 80? score \+ 1**  
  - **stress tolerance \>= 60? score \+ 1**  
  - **communication \>= 80? score \+ 2**  
- **In divorce, you keep everything (house, car, kids)**  
  - **Divorce finalizes in following year (remind them of anything they need to take care of like new car)**  
  - **You pick which car (yours or spouse’s you want to keep)**  
    - **If doesn’t fit whole fam, must sell and get new car**  
  - **Give notification to change childcare, can’t checkout until childcare meets needs**  
  - **When divorce, split up money, retirement savings, loans**  
    - **\*CODE: store yours and spouse’s original money (minus loans), savings, loans**  
    - **In divorce, check current money, savings, loans ($):**  
      - **Subtract yours \+ spouse’s original $ (and loans) from current $, divide by 2, then add your original $ back and that’s your portion**  
    - **^ same for retirement savings**  
  - **\+10% stress**  
- **Spouse comes with transportation of varying age**  
- **Spouse dies when player does**  
- **Spouse is same age as you**  
- **Spouse comes in with money/savings depending on age:**  
  - **Their money goes into your money, their savings go into your retirement account, their debt goes into your loans**  
  - **Retirement savings (how much savings spouse entered marriage with \- calculate spouse savings based on their job’s salary starting from starting salary through current year \+ associated inflation)**  
    - **\<=$35000 salary: 1-5%/yr**  
    - **\<=$70000 salary: 3-10%/yr**  
    - **\<=$105000 salary: 5-15%/yr**  
    - **\<=$150000 salary: 15-20%/yr**  
  - **Money (how much money spouse entered marriage with)**  
    - **Total earnings (calculate what salary would have been each year (including inflation) and add it up) \- savings \- X% total earnings**  
    - **If current salary \<=$35000, X \= 90**  
    - **If current salary \<=$70000, X \= 80**  
    - **If current salary \<=$105000, X \= 60**  
    - **If current salary \<=$150000, X \= 50**  
  - **Spouse Debt: add up cost of all degrees obtained by spouse \* X**  
    - **Roll 5-sided die:**   
      - **1: X \= 1**  
      - **2: X \= 0.75**  
      - **3: X \= 0.50**  
      - **4: X \= 0.25**  
      - **5: X \= 0**  
    - **Age \<= 35: reduce orig. debt by 25%**  
    - **Age \<= 40: reduce orig. debt by 50%**  
    - **Age \<= 45: reduce orig. debt by 75%**  
    - **Age \<= 50: reduce orig. debt by 100%**

## **PETS**

- **Can buy small or large pet**  
- **Pets take 2 time blocks (no matter how many you have)**  
- **Yearly pet stress: 1%/small pet, 2%/large pet**  
- **Randomly dies between ages 4-6 (small) or 9-11 (big)**

## **HOUSES**

- **Coding note: track how long had current house**  
- **Two areas: city and suburbs**  
- **Cost 1 time blocks commute to job if live in other area (if have two jobs (PT/FT) in other area, still only costs 1 time block (no commute for both))**  
  - **Does not cost a time block to go to school (university is between city and suburbs)**  
- **At beginning of game, player is told when their parents expect them to be out of the house by**  
  - **Reminders**  
  - **Enforced \- have to buy a house by that age, cannot do end of year without moving out**  
- **If more people than recommended max, more stressed**  
  - **\+2 stress/extra person**  
- **Cannot go over legal max people in housing**  
- **Communal housing: parents’ place, college dorm, room for rent**  
- **If have pool, sell house for additional 10% of current value**  
- **If have solar panels, sell for additional 10% of current value**  
- **Insurance (optional)**  
  - **See Houses sheet**  
- **Compare feature on actions page**  
- **If you try to move and you can’t fit all your pets in your new house, show notice that they’ll have to give extra pets up for adoption**  
- **‘Add to Cart’ button and then pop up to select any applicable options such as location and whether they want insurance**  
- **Show current home value to player**

## **TRANSPORTATION**

- **Account for you and spouses’ cars**  
- **Insurance mandatory**  
  - **Factors: Person’s age (inverted bell curve), car age, original cost of vehicle, green vehicle discount**  
  - **Person’s age: 18-20 yrs \= 5, 21-25 yrs \= 4, 26-35 \= 3, 36-45 \= 2, 46-55 \= 1, 56-60 \= 2, 61-65 \= 3, 66-70 \= 4, 71+ \= 5**  
  - **Car age: 0-2 yrs \= 5, 3-4 yrs \= 4, 5-7 \= 3, 8-10 \= 2, 11+ \= 1**  
  - **Cost of vehicle: $0-19999 \= 1, $20000-39999 \= 2, 40000-59999 \= 3, 60000-79999 \= 4, 80000-100000 \= 5**  
  - **Insurance cost (average all factors and round):**  
    - **Insurance cost: 1 \= $800, 2 \= 1200, 3 \= 1500, 4 \= 2200, 5 \= 3000**  
    - **Green vehicle discount: 5% off for hybrids, 10% off for electric**  
    - **If accident this year:**  
      - **Multiply by 50% for current yr if at-fault**  
      - **Multiply by 20% for current yr if not at-fault**  
- **car gets older over time so need to buy new one, can get a used car that has less years left on it (and more likely car probs and waste action on buy new car more often) but costs less**  
- **Two variables to track car’s age: “Car age” is car’s actual age in years (ex: how long you’ve had it) (this is what we show to players), “car health” is car’s age but also takes into account how many miles you’ve put on it \- the more you drive, the less years your car lasts (only track this behind-the-scenes, don’t show to players)**  
  - **If commute is 0 time blocks, car health \= car age**  
  - **If commute is 1 time blocks, car health \= car age \* 1.35**  
- **Car may die when it gets old:**  
  - **Car health 11-15: 50% chance/yr**  
  - **Car health 16+: 100%**  
- **When car died, notify player, remove car from their profile info, and let them know they need to get a new car this year and they can’t check out if they don’t have a new car that meets their needs in their cart**  
- **yearly car maintenance fees, (don’t tell players but gets more expensive the older the car is \= incentive to get new car)**  
  - **0-2 \= 1100, 3-4 \= $1200, 5-7 \= 1300, 8-10 \= 1400, 11+ \= 1500**  
- **Compare feature on actions page**  
- **‘Add to Cart’ button and then pop up to select any applicable options such as location and whether they want insurance**  
- **At least one transportation in the family must fit everyone**   
- **If both parents’ work overlaps, need 2 forms of transportation unless whole family has public transit**  
- **Need to get new bike after 5 yrs**  
  - **Cannot travel between city/sub with bike so enforce that can’t get job/house in diff locations unless get diff transportation**  
- **Depreciation calculation:**  
  - **Bike: worth goes down $100/yr \= $0 in last yr**  
  - **Motorcycle: 15%/yr for 0-5 yrs, 5%/yr for 6+ yrs**  
  - **Car:**

    **A \= P \* (1 \- R/100)^n**

    **D \= P \- A**

    

    **A is the value of the car after n years,**

    **D is the depreciation amount,**

    **P is the purchase amount,**

    **R is the percentage rate of depreciation per annum (14%),**

    **n is car age.**

    

## **CARDS**

- **Every year, each player may or may not get a card after every action (20% chance of getting a card after express checkout or putting action in card), up to 3 cards per person per year**  
- **On cards, give explanations of why it happened/suggestions on how to avoid it in the future**  
- **Can only have max two chronic health conditions (if start game with one, can only pick chronic health condition card once)**  
- **In bike crash, always driver’s fault so their insurance pays you the amount of a new bike (but player has to do new transportation action to get new bike)**  
- **Can only pick specific vehicle cards if you have that vehicle**  
- **Take into account spouse’s vehicle for “car problem” cars (if spouse \= old car, more likely car probs)**

## **ACTIONS**

- **On Expenses page, have a section for actions. Button takes you to Actions page where you pick out actions, add them to your cart, and then “go to checkout” which redirects back to Expenses page with the actions section filled in accordingly. Paying for actions takes effect when you pay all your expenses at end of year.**   
  - **Note: include potential skill/stress/health/money gains/losses to determine eligibility/affordability for things**  
- **For actions that go into cart, can either do Express Checkout or Add to Cart (once added to cart, still has a button for Express Checkout)**  
  - **Express Checkout happens immediately (all effects of action happen)**  
- **For actions that always happen immediately, only have Express Checkout option**  
- **Required actions**  
  - **If just had child and need bigger house/car, recommend buy house/buy car**  
  - **CPR certification if just expired (if job requires it)**  
- **Recommended actions**  
  - **Stress**  
    - **If high stress (\>= 70%), recommend actions that reduce stress**  
    - **If high stress (\>= 70%), do not recommend actions that increase stress**  
  - **Health**  
    - **If low health (\<= 40%), recommend actions that increase health**  
  - **Job actions (recommend eligible ones if they’ve done one in that job category)**  
    - **Actor: acting class, commercial, tv show, movie**  
    - **Musician:**   
      - **Perform at bar**  
      - **Perform at school dance**  
      - **Perform at county fair**  
      - **Release EP**  
      - **Open for tour headliner**  
      - **Release album**  
      - **Headline national tour**  
      - **Headline international tour**  
    - **author: write a book, self-publish, submit to publisher**  
  - **Teach summer courses for teacher and professor**  
  - **If in college:**  
    - **apply for scholarships**  
    - **do an internship**  
    - **do study abroad**  
  - **If grandparent, spend time and money on grandkids**  
  - **Recycle**  
  - **If car is old (11+ yrs car health), buy a new car**  
- **Looks like aisles in grocery store with actions on shelves**  
- **Make sure users are aware that 10 time blocks/year are for sleeping \- they can only use those time blocks for other actions if they earn them back through stay up late action**  
- **Lock items in cart that need to do this year**  
- **When looking through actions, disable (but still display) actions you are not eligible for until the actions in your cart will make you eligible (ex: you don’t meet the health reqs for an action so add a health gain action and the original action you wanted)**  
  - **If you take out the action that made you eligible for the other action, give a warning that you won’t be able to do the other action**  
- **CODE: execute actions side effects in this order:**  
  - **Gain skills/traits/health/stress**  
  - **Gain money**  
  - **Everything else (including paying)**  
- **Things you can’t remove from shopping cart:**  
  - **job**  
  - **childcare**  
  - **spouse**  
- **Determine if got into school at end of year (based on school reqs)**  
- **If your money \- actions cost \= negative number, you get a warning that you don’t have enough money and you might want to get a loan or do actions that earn you money**  
  - **Include drawer or something to view expenses from actions page so can make sure actions cost is within budget (also show cart total at bottom of cart)**  
- **Remodel home (only for owned homes)**  
  - **Player chooses amount to put into remodel (X)**  
  - **Home Value Increase min \= X × 0.5**  
  - **Home Value Increase max \= X × 0.8**  
  - **Calculate (Home Value Increase min) and (Home Value Increase max), determine percentage of current home value those are \=\> Y-Z% possible increase in home value due to remodel**  
- **Spouse school (send them to get next degree in their education interest)**  
  - **You pick FT or PT**  
  - **Show impacts (salary, childcare, etc)**  
- **Spouse job (change FT or PT) \- spouse can only have 1 job (even if PT)**  
  - **If also in school, both must be PT (automatic change, give them a warning)**  
- **Divorce**  
  - **Action only enabled if compatibility score is \<= 3**  
  - **Cost of divorce depends on how low compatibility score is:**  
    - **score \= 3: costs 1 time block and $500**  
    - **score \= 2: costs 1 time block and $10000**  
    - **score \= 1: costs 2 time block and $20000**  
    - **score \= 0: costs 3 time block and $100000**  
  - **show results (i.e. childcare costs increase, etc.)**  
- **Toggle childcare**  
  - **Include button for popup that shows the childcare cost charts, highlight the option that is best for them if they want to save time blocks and option that is for them if they want to save money**  
  - **Dropdown to select only show them the options that meet or exceed their needs, these are the options:**  
    - **Year Childcare FT**  
    - **Year Childcare PT**  
    - **Year Childcare PT \+ Summer Childcare FT**  
    - **Summer Childcare FT**  
    - **Summer Childcare PT**  
  - **Taking care of your kids**  
    - **Day divided into three sections: Morning, afternoon, evening**  
    - **Increased time blocks used and higher stress with more kids**  
    - **If both parents \= part-time, work different sections of the day**  
    - **If both parents full-time or one FT and one PT, need childcare**  
    - **Parent availability includes parent work and school**  
    - **Childcare:**   
      - **When have child, pop-up shows them the childcare chart with recommended options and they select new one**  
        - **Don’t ask every year, if they want to change it they have to change it on the childcare item in their cart**  
        - **When important changes happen, pop-up shows them the childcare chart with recommended options and they select new one**  
          - **Ex: When child starts school (at age 3), when you or spouse change job FT/PT, etc**  
        - **Stops automatically when child is old enough not to need it anymore (age 13\) (have pop-up informing them of this)**  
      - **Cost:**  
        - **Year FT: $12000/yr/child**  
        - **Year PT: $6000/yr/child**  
        - **Year PT  \+ Summer FT: $7500/yr/child**  
        - **Summer FT: $3000/yr/child**  
        - **Summer PT: $1500/yr/child**  
      - **reduces “taking care of kids” time blocks by what the “taking care of kids” time blocks would have been**   
- **Try to have a child**  
  - **Must be \<= 45 years old**  
  - **Roll 100-sided die:**  
    - **Under age 30: 95% chance**  
    - **Age 31-35: 80% chance**  
    - **Age 36-40: 60% chance**  
    - **Age 41-45: 25% chance**  
  - **Tell players less likely to get pregnant as they get older and disable action once reach max age**  
  - **If got pregnant, get notified of that and number of babies (roll: 5% chance of twins, 1% chance of triplets, 94% chance of singleton) and any actions you need to take care of before child is born (upsize their car/house), also tell them how much their cost for birth will be**  
  - **Next year at beginning of year:**  
    - **player gets notified whether gave birth successfully (per child, e.g., if twins then roll for each child’s success)**  
      - **Under age 30: 97% chance of success**  
      - **Age 31-35: 93% chance of success**  
      - **Age 36-40: 85% chance of success**  
      - **Age 41-45: 60% chance of success**  
    - **Calculate costs: add up cost for each baby (using the cost from either successful or stillborn for each baby below)**  
      - **Without health insurance, pay $20000 for singleton, $50000/baby for twins, $125000/baby for triplets (for next year if birth successful)**  
      - **With health insurance, pay $3000 for singleton, $2500/baby for twins, $2250/baby for triplets (for next year if birth successful)**  
      - **Stillbirth without insurance: $30000/baby**  
      - **Stillbirth with insurance: $7500/baby**  
  - **If need a bigger house/car, need to do it before end of year the year you give birth (i.e., you have year you find out you are pregnant \+ 1 year to upgrade things)**  
  - **Gain X lemons/yr/child for rest of life**  
    - **If good with kids \< 40%, get 2 lemons**  
    - **Else if good with kids \< 80, get 3 lemons**  
    - **Else get 4 lemons**  
  - **Build 2% patience/yr, 2% compassion/yr, 2% organization/yr, 2% caution/yr, 2% good with kids/yr**  
  - **Stress increases 4%/time block you take care of the kid(s)**  
  - **If unsuccessful at getting pregnant, stress increases 2%**  
  - **Can do this action as many times as desired in the same year UNTIL you are successful, then it’s disabled for that year**  
- **Apply to adopt child**  
  - **Can only submit one application per year, action is then disabled until you get a child from that application**  
  - **Can select one desired age group (only if you meet that age group’s requirement)**  
    - **Child age 0-2:**  
      - **Requirements**  
        - **stress \<= 50**  
        - **you must be \<= 42 age**  
      - **Adoption Process**  
        - **child available to adopt randomly within 5 yrs (this year \+ 4, pick random number 1-5 skewed left and that is the year the child is available, tell them it’s “typically within 6 yrs” so they don’t now for sure on 5th yr), notify player when child available and they have option to go through with adoption for following year but must still meet requirements by end of this year, next year adoption goes through and costs 2 time blocks**  
    - **Child age 3-9:**  
      - **Requirements**  
        - **stress \<= 60**  
        - **you must be \<= 50 age**  
      - **Adoption Process**  
        - **child available to adopt within 1 yr (this year), costs 2 time blocks that year**  
    - **Child age 10-17:**  
      - **Requirements**  
        - **stress \<= 60**  
        - **you must be \<= 55 age**  
      - **Adoption Process**  
        - **child available to adopt within 1 yr (this year), costs 2 time blocks that year**  
- **Find love**  
  - **After get married, auto-put a Spouse item in your cart**  
    - **Spouse item includes everything about them that can be adjusted, ex: school, job, car**  
  - **Spouses don’t come with kids**  
  - **Reminder about your share of the wedding cost**  
  - **Dating app-style action:**  
    - **All candidates are same age as you**  
    - **You must have \>= 50 communication**  
    - **You must have \>= 50 compassion**  
    - **You can input desired characteristics:**  
      - **Money**  
      - **Salary**  
      - **Type of vehicle**  
      - **Type of housing**  
      - **PTO**  
      - **Education interest (major)**  
    - **Candidate profile:**  
      - **Job info (from jobs page, ex: salary, raise, degrees, PTO) OR RETIRED if you are over age 65**  
      - **Money (include loans, have note that money includes loans)**  
      - **Savings**  
      - **Loans**  
      - **Same age as you**  
      - **Type of vehicle**  
      - **Type of housing**  
      - **Education interest**  
      - **Calculate initial compatibility score:**  
        - **Calculate your portion of the score based on (stress, compassion, patience, stress tolerance, communication) from annual compatibility formula**  
        - **Add random (0-2) for candidate’s score**   
      - **\* when randomly generate spouse, can’t be actor, author, ride-share driver, singer**  
    - **Player presented with 3 candidates**  
      - **Candidates will each match at least one criteria (randomly generated)**  
      - **Restrict possible degrees based on age. 40% chance candidate does not have degree at all, 60% chance candidate has a degree they are eligible for given their age below:**  
        - **\<=20: high school diploma**  
        - **\>=20, CC (remember that if they go to get bachelors later, since same major, only 2 yrs more)**  
        - **\>=22: bachelors**  
        - **\>=24: masters**  
        - **\>=30: PhD**  
* **Spouse won’t have vocational/professional schools**  
  - **If do dating app and don’t select spouse, costs 2 time blocks, get 1 lemons**  
    - **If do dating app and get married, costs 6 time blocks, get 3 lemons**  
    - **Wedding costs $30,000, each spouse pays half (so your cost is $15,000, see parents’ contribution for how much they pay of your half)**  
      - **Spouse’s parents always pay their half \- they will never not have enough money for their half**  
      - **When remarrying, your parents don’t help pay for wedding and cost lowered to $15000 total (split with spouse)**  
    - **When get married, combine savings and money**  
    - **While married, build 1% communication, compassion, and patience each year (max 20% each gained across all marriages)**  
      - **\[CODE\] track total num yrs married in game (all marriages)**  
    - **Show candidates’ housing age**  
    - **\*CODE: for dating app, construct candidate profiles in real-time based on user’s input criteria, query DB for jobs that meet criteria (major, salary, degree level, etc), then randomly generate 3 candidates from there**  
    - **Player can select one of the candidates or pass.**   
      - **If they select a candidate, marriage item goes in their cart. Actually get married the next year (so you are single this year). Results (traits gained, wedding costs, etc) happen at checkout, not immediately. Find Love action is disable while marriage item is in cart**  
      - **If they don’t select a candidate, they can do the Find Love action again that year.**  
- **Buy transportation**  
  - **Select if it’s for you or spouse**  
  - **Select whether want to sell old transportation  (if applicable, i.e., not public transit or bike)**  
- **Buy housing**  
  - **Select whether want to sell old housing (if applicable, i.e., not rented or parents’ place)**  
- **Shopping cart and checkout system to pick year’s actions, at checkout can decide to use some/all of PTO (domestic and international vacations and mental health days always require PTO)**  
  - **If all time blocks used and add something to cart, pop-up asks if want to use PTO for it if they have PTO, otherwise pop-up tells them they already used all their time blocks**  
  - **If all time blocks used and no PTO (or not enough) available, cannot put more actions in cart if require time blocks**  
  - **If then take something out of cart (and have free time block(s)), action using PTO automatically switches to using time blocks (tell player this is happening)**  
  - **1 time block \= 5 days PTO**  
- **For jobs that require CPR certification, when it expires, you won’t be able to check out until you put CPR certification in your cart, tell them this is the reason they cannot check out**  
- **School**  
  - **Drop out of school**  
    - **If living in dorm, need to move out, so show pop-up to select new housing**  
  - **Change major**  
    - **CODE: track what schooling they’ve completed to see if they already meet reqs for another major/field**  
- **Senior discount for ages 65+**  
  - **Applies to self and spouse**  
- **You have to use all of your available time blocks (if you have extra, do the relax action)**  
- **Receive badges for actions and other important events/milestones (graduating school, retiring, etc) (one per action for lifetime)**  
- **Pets:**  
  - **Adopt a pet: Buy pet (large or small), Sections for big and small, each has dropdown for number (max pets based on house)**  
  - **Put pet up for adoption: checklist to pick which ones (labels are pet size, pet age)**  
- **\[CODE\] For actions that can do multiple in a yr and result is based on skills (like write book, do acting job, etc.) handle as though separate actions to check skill level each time (ex: wrote 3 books, handle as “wrote a book” \- check skills for salary, “wrote a book” \- check skills for salary, “wrote a book” \- check skills for salary)**  
- **Separate actions in cart for each job (job types: FT, PT, summer)**  
  - **Summer jobs can be part-time or full-time**  
  - **When try to get a job,**   
    - **Do express checkout on find a job action (select desired job from searchable dropdown, checkbox to hide ineligible jobs \- default is checked)**  
      - **Notification on get a job action that jobs you previously had and need continuing education to get back are not currently eligible**  
        - **If enough continuing education is in cart, job becomes eligible**  
        - **If get job, continuing education is locked in cart, cannot be removed**  
    - **Then determine whether they got the job based on the following:**  
      - **Check “Ease of Getting” ranking from spreadsheet:**  
        - **For 100% \= 100% chance**  
        - **For Custom, use the criteria specified for the job**  
        - **Otherwise, roll to see if they got it:**  
          - **1: 50% chance of success (\~4 tb)**  
          - **2: 40% chance of success (\~6 tb)**  
          - **3: 30% chance of success (\~8 tb)**  
          - **4: 20% chance of success (\~10 tb)**  
          - **5: 10% chance of success (\~20 tb)**  
    - **If got job, job item goes in cart (stays there year to year unless lose job or decide to quit by manually removing it) \- each job is a separate item in cart, can have at most 3 PT’s worth of jobs in your cart (ex: 1 FT and 1PT, or 3 PT) at end of year, so if have too many jobs then can’t checkout until remove some**  
    - **School-year based jobs (teacher, professor, dean, school counselor, principal) don’t work during summer \=\> don’t count them as having that job FT during summer (3 months \= 4 TB) (i.e., they can have 3 other PT’s worth of job in summer)**  
    - **Show on jobs page how long it typically takes in time blocks to get each job**  
    - **If got job, takes effect immediately (not at end of year) (i.e., use up time block immediately and get paid that year)**  
    - **If didn’t get job, they can try again that year**  
  - **Rideshare driver: alert them if they are within 2 yrs of max car age requirement**  
  - **Job(s) automatically appears in shopping cart each year (if have a job)**  
  - **To switch from FT to PT or vice versa, switch drop-down in job action in cart**  
    - **When switching your or spouse’s job PT → FT and have kids \< 18, check if childcare needs to be updated and give them notification**  
  - **Quit job**  
    - **Remove from cart**  
    - **Show notification that they will have to do continuing education if they want to get the job back, are you sure you want to quit?**  
  - **If you or spouse does not have job when checking out (only the year they first took the job out of their cart), ask if they are sure they don’t want to get a new job**  
  - **Spouse automatically retires at age 65 if you have not retired them yet**  
  - **If trying to get old job (that requires schooling)**  
    - **Show new salary, etc**  
    - **Show that if \>= 2 yrs since last had that job, must do continuing education**  
      - **Costs 2 time blocks per 2 yrs since last had that job, max of 20 time blocks**  
- **Academic actions all separate, can decide which ones to do each year**  
  - **If don’t have enough academic actions in cart at checkout, have pop-up telling them that to get 100% of skills/traits for that school year, must spend X time blocks on academic actions (tell them how much they currently have in cart)**  
- **Pursue education**  
  - **All in one action**  
    - **Dropdowns to select type of school (other, community college, 4-year college, masters program, PhD program) and field, after field is picked another dropdown pops up to pick major within that field**  
    - **Vocational, Associates and bachelors always available (might go back for different degree), masters, professional, and PhD only available after completed applicable prerequisite degree**  
  - **When do express checkout, if don’t get into school (don’t meet requirements), get notified immediately and can go back into pursue education action to do different program (ex. Applied for bachelors but didn’t get in → go back into education action and apply for community college or vocational school)**  
- **Change FT/PT school action**  
  - **Put description on action about effects (tuition, academic action time blocks, etc)**  
  - **Action says “You are currently attending school \_\_\_\_\_-Time. Switch to \_\_\_\_\_-Time.”**  
- **CPR certification:**  
  - **Get a badge**  
  - **Expires, must recertify every 2 years**  
- **Different types of unpaid internships \- give different traits/skills**  
  - **Available at all degree levels**  
  - **Action has dropdown to pick which internship they want to do**  
  - **Humanities**  
    - **Communication: 8%**  
    - **Sociability: 10%**  
    - **Compassion: 10%**  
    - **Patience: 10%**  
    - **Charisma: 10%**  
    - **Organization: 2%**  
    - **Creativity: 8%**  
  - **Math**  
    - **Math: 5%**  
    - **Analysis: 2%**  
    - **Organization: 2%**  
    - **Communication: 2%**  
  - **Science**  
    - **Science: 5%**  
    - **Analysis: 2%**  
    - **Organization: 2%**   
    - **Caution: 3%**  
    - **Technology: 1%**  
    - **Communication: 2%**  
  - **Technology**  
    - **Technology: 5%**  
    - **Organization: 2%**  
    - **Communication: 2%**  
    - **Creativity: 3%**
